verbGlossary.ASSIGNED TO: {source} instrument is allocated to {target} within the project (e.g., subcontract assigned to Building A)
verbGlossary.SCOPED TO: {source} work scope is limited to {target} or level within the building
verbGlossary.DELIVERED TO: {source} materials are shipped to a designated laydown or {target} on site
verbGlossary.BOUNDED BY: {source} is geographically limited to {target}, wing, or exterior area of the project
verbGlossary.DELINEATED BY: {source} scope is organized and separated by {target} or floor to define clear work boundaries for bidders
verbGlossary.COVERS: {source} instrument (warranty, insurance) provides coverage for {target} or functional area
verbGlossary.CERTIFIED FOR: {source} is officially certified for {target} or building, enabling partial occupancy or turnover
verbGlossary.ACCEPTED AT: Final completion acceptance occurs at the project {target} level, confirming all contractual obligations are met
verbGlossary.EXTENDS TO: {source} insurance coverage extends to temporary facilities, trailers, and stored materials on {target}
verbGlossary.ASSESSED PER: {source} are calculated per work {target} or phase area when that zone misses its contractual completion date
verbGlossary.SUBMITTED FOR: {source} is assembled and submitted for a specific commissioning {target}
verbGlossary.SEGMENTED BY: {source} is broken down by {target} or floor for comparative analysis across project locations
verbGlossary.ENFORCED AT: {source} requirements are enforced at the project {target} level per jurisdictional labor regulations
verbGlossary.SECURED FOR: {source} secures the project {target}, guaranteeing contractual obligations will be fulfilled at that location
